What JC² Means

Jere + Jesica, multiplied

JC² means Jere and Jesica brought together and elevated. The squared mark signals that this is bigger than two initials — it is love, commitment, and shared purpose multiplied.

One identity, one direction

It represents a marriage with its own name, voice, and presence. Not two separate stories sitting side by side, but one identity moving forward together.

The flame and 10/10 mean commitment

The mark is tied directly to your wedding day. It turns October 10 into more than a date — it becomes the symbol of the day your story became official.

A brand for the life you are building

JC² is made to carry the full story: marriage, family, milestones, reels, memories, fitness, and future chapters. It is personal, rooted, and built to last.

Our Story

It started with history, not chance

Jere and Jesica’s story carries more weight because it did not begin with a random introduction. There was already history, familiarity, and something real underneath it all long before the wedding day ever existed.

One invitation opened the door

A comedy show invite became the moment everything shifted. What looked simple on the surface turned into a reconnection with real depth, real peace, and real momentum almost immediately.

Forty days from reconnection to marriage

The pace became part of the beauty of the story. It was fast because it was clear. In forty days, the two of you went from reconnecting to becoming husband and wife, trusting what felt right and believing it was God’s work bringing it together.

10/10/25 became the anchor

Marriage was not the ending of the story. It was the official beginning of your shared life — one home, one direction, one identity. From Georgetown forward, the story has been about building a real life together, and JC² became the symbol of that bond.

This section is built to feel bigger and more visual. It includes official Instagram embed slots plus a styled fallback showcase so the page still looks strong even before specific reel URLs are dropped in.

To make the true Instagram embeds work, replace the three placeholder reel URLs with your actual reel links. Instagram does not support embedding a full profile feed in a single iframe, so the best working approach is embedding specific reels and pairing them with a styled gallery section like this.
